items.briefing:
Cybercriminals are abusing ChatGPT's shared chat links to host fake outage pages that trick users into downloading malware. If you receive a link to a ChatGPT page — particularly one claiming the service is down — do not click it or download anything it offers. This tactic is concerning for staff who regularly use AI tools, as the links can appear convincing and legitimate.

There are no major Australia-specific incidents to report today. Activity on Scamwatch remains ongoing, so remain cautious of unsolicited emails, texts, or calls requesting personal or financial information.

**Tip:** Before clicking any shared link, verify it comes from a trusted source you were expecting to hear from.

featured.title: Australia's sovereign AI adoption stays governance-led
featured.quote: Sovereign AI adoption critical as Australian agencies struggle with security and privacy governance challenges.
